rulings.Case Title: KENNETH LUI VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, A PUBLIC ENTITY et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, August 23, 2010, line 15. DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is denied. Defendant fails to provide a satisfactory explanation why these defenses were not raised in the first motion for summary judgment. Motion also denied on its merits. To determine whether or not plaintiff can safely perform his job, the court must determine the essential functions of his job. As the court previously ruled based on Cripe v. City of San Jose 261 F. 3d 877, there is a triable issue of fact regarding the essential functions of plaintiff's job. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: JOHN HICKS et al VS. FRANK YUEN et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, August 23, 2010, line 2. CROSS DEFENDANTS JOHN HICKS, NEILL AMICK, ZACH TAYLOR'S MOTION TO STRIKE CROSS COMPLAINT is denied, 10 days leave to answer. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: JOHN HICKS et al VS. FRANK YUEN et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, August 23, 2010, line 2. CROSS DEFENDANTS JOHN HICKS, NEILL AMICK, ZACH TAYLOR'S DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT is sustained without leave to amend as to 7th - 12th causes of action for indemnity and contribution. The doctrine of indemnity only applies among defendants who are jointly and severally liable to the plaintiffs. 5 Witkin Summary 10th (2005) Torts section 115, p. 212. A right to contribution exists by statute in California "where a money judgment has been rendered jointly against one or more defendants in a tort action..." C.C.P. section 875(a). No reasonable probability that defects can be cured by amendment. Demurrer overruled in all other respects, 10 days leave to answer. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: DOMENIC J CANNIZZARO et al VS. FRED P RICHARD et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, August 23, 2010, line 3. CROSS DEFENDANT FRED RICHARD'S DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT is sustained with 10 days leave to amend as to the 3rd cause of action, breach of contract. Cross complainant does not allege that cross-defendant Fred Richard was a party to the contract, and therefore he may not be liable for the breach. Cross-complainant may amend to allege a cause of action regarding the contract that is supported by the allegations. Demurrer overruled as to remaining causes of action. Although there is a substantial similarity between this case and the other law suit, the parties and causes of action are not identical. Cross-defendant may raise the issue of another action pending as an affirmative defense. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: SOMMERS & SCHWARTZ LLP et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Petition To Confirm Attorney-Client Fee Arb Award
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, August 23, 2010, line 8. PETITIONERS SOMMERS & SCHWARTZ LLP, LAW OFFICES OF PAUL J. STEINER'S Petition To Confirm Attorney-Client Fee Arbitration Award is granted. Respondent does not present any grounds why the award should be vacated or modified pursuant to C.C.P. section 1286.2 or C.C.P. section 1286.6. The award allows for post-award interest. The court does not adjudicate the outstanding costs and lienholder issues at this time. The lienholders have not been given notice of this proceeding, and no competent evidence presented to substantiate the alleged outstanding costs. =(302/CWW)
